stimulus.content: Police come to a man's house and want to search inside. The man tells them they need a warrant first. He says the Fourth Amendment gives him this right.

stem: Why does the Fourth Amendment require police to get a warrant before a search?

choices.text: To keep people safe in their homes
To help police do their jobs faster
To make sure crimes are not reported
To let judges run the police force

answer_explanation: The Fourth Amendment protects people from unreasonable searches and seizures. It requires a warrant so that people can feel safe and secure in their homes. It is not about helping police work faster or giving judges control over police.
